rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical four-component 1,2-difunctionalization of arylalkenes with carboxylic acids, acetonitrile, and alcohols to give functionalized imides via in situ O-acyl isoamide formation and Mumm rearrangement
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of styrene to a radical cation initiates the cascade; cathodic reduction of methanol and benzoic acid generates methoxide and carboxylate nucleophiles plus H2

key_intermediates.name: nitrilium ion D (Ritter-type) from MeCN trapping

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes styrene to radical cation A; methoxide (from cathodic reduction of MeOH) adds to give benzyl radical B, which is further oxidized at the anode to cation C; MeCN traps C as nitrilium D, which is captured by carboxylate (from cathodic reduction of ArCOOH) to form O-acyl isoamide E that undergoes 1,3-(O->N) Mumm rearrangement to the imide.
rate_determining_step: Not the methoxy or acetonitrile incorporation step (excluded by KIE experiments); likely initial anodic oxidation of styrene

probe_reagents.name: CD3OD
probe_reagents.observation: Parallel reaction with CH3OH/CD3OD (1:1) gave 3ao/d3-3ao = 1:1, indicating methoxy incorporation is not rate-determining

probe_reagents.name: CD3CN
probe_reagents.observation: Parallel reaction with CH3CN/CD3CN (1:1) gave 3ao/d3-3ao = 1:1, indicating MeCN incorporation is not rate-determining

probe_reagents.name: 18O-labeled 4-chlorobenzoic acid
probe_reagents.observation: 18O is incorporated into the imide oxygen of [18O]-3ak, confirming the acyl-O-to-N migration (Mumm rearrangement) pathway

notes: CV showed styrene has the lowest oxidation potential (1.60 V vs SCE), supporting direct anodic oxidation of the alkene rather than the acid or alcohol as the initiating event. The key novelty is electrochemical generation of an otherwise unstable O-acyl isoamide intermediate that spontaneously undergoes Mumm 1,3-acyl O->N migration.
